sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: News | End of an era: Why a Northern California town is mourning the loss of its Taco Bell
feed.summary: A Northern California town is mourning the closure of its local Taco Bell, which operated for nearly 60 years and was one of the chain's last remaining mission-style outlets. The restaurant, located in Lafayette, was an unofficial town landmark and symbolized a vanished era of distinctive fast-food architecture. Residents reminisced about their personal memories associated with the restaurant, highlighting its role beyond just serving food.

feed.title: How Dividend Affirmation and Revenue Expectations At SLM (SLM) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Sallie Mae (SLM) recently declared its Q1 2026 dividends for both preferred and common stock, coinciding with anticipation for its Q4 2025 earnings release where analysts expect renewed year-on-year revenue growth. This dividend affirmation reinforces management's commitment to shareholder distributions despite share price weakness and ongoing legal actions related to delinquencies. The article explores how this combination of dividend consistency and expected revenue recovery shapes SLM's investment narrative, while also noting risks related to credit quality and legal scrutiny.

feed.title: Form DEF 14A Cencora, Inc. For: Mar 05
feed.summary: Cencora, Inc. has filed its definitive proxy statement (Form DEF 14A) for its Annual Meeting of Shareholders scheduled for March 5, 2026. The filing includes details on the company's fiscal 2025 performance, which saw $321.3 billion in revenue and a 40% total shareholder return, driven by its pharmaceutical-centric strategy and the acquisition of Retina Consultants of America. Key proposals for the meeting include the election of 11 director nominees, an advisory vote on fiscal 2025 executive compensation, and the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2026.

feed.title: F5 (FFIV) CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. has filed a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (FFIV) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5 made misleading statements and concealed a significant security breach, which negatively impacted its security capabilities and market value. Investors have until February 17, 2026, to apply for lead plaintiff status.

feed.title: Kenvue stock price rebounds as merger vote nears — what KVUE traders watch next
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. (KVUE) shares rebounded by 2.2% to $17.77 as investors anticipate a merger vote with Kimberly-Clark (KMB) on January 29. The deal is valued at approximately $18.40 per Kenvue share, creating a merger-arbitrage spread. Traders are closely watching Kimberly-Clark's earnings report on January 27, as it could significantly impact the deal's implied value and the spread.

feed.title: FDIC clears way for Ford, General Motors to set up industrial banks
feed.summary: The FDIC has granted conditional approval for Ford Motor Company and General Motors to establish industrial banks, allowing them to offer certain financial services. This decision is part of an ongoing dispute between traditional banks, which oppose such charters due to concerns about oversight, and proponents who believe they enable limited financial service provision to customers. The auto manufacturers must set up their lending arms within 12 months.

feed.title: Ground Broken On $38M Crawfordsville Kroger Marketplace
feed.summary: Kroger has broken ground on a new $38 million Kroger Marketplace and fuel center in Crawfordsville, Montgomery County, Indiana. This 123,000 square foot store will be the grocer's largest format in the area, offering new specialties like Murray’s Cheese, Starbucks, and fresh sushi, along with a wide selection of apparel. The investment is part of Kroger Central Division's $200 million year for new construction and expansions.

feed.title: Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. (SFM) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Howard G. Smith announced an opportunity for Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. (SFM) shareholders who suffered losses to lead a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that between June 4, 2025, and October 29, 2025, Sprouts made materially misleading statements regarding its customer base, resilience to macroeconomic environments, and sales growth trajectory. Investors who incurred losses during this period are encouraged to contact the firm before the January 26, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline.

feed.title: Feds approve GM, Ford industrial banks for vehicle savings accounts
feed.summary: General Motors and Ford have received conditional federal approval to establish industrial banks, allowing them to accept FDIC-insured deposits for vehicle-related savings. This move aims to help consumers with vehicle affordability amidst rising transaction prices. The banks, chartered in Utah, will offer savings accounts, and potentially certificates of deposit and indirect financing, seeking to strengthen customer relationships and lower the automakers' funding costs.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group raises quarterly dividend by 6.7 percent
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group announced a 6.7% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to 95.25 cents per share, payable March 1, 2026, marking its 23rd consecutive year of dividend increases. The utility company has a long history of dividend payments, maintains a dividend payout target of 65-70% of earnings, and has received updated price targets from analysts at Jefferies, BofA Securities, and KeyBanc.

feed.title: PED Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: PEDEVCO Corp. (PED) is an energy company focused on oil and natural gas projects in the U.S., particularly in the Permian Basin and Rockies. A significant development was the North Peak Merger, which expanded its Rockies assets to over 320,000 net acres with 6,500 BOEPD production. The company's business model involves acquiring and developing high-growth projects, managing operations, and utilizing both equity and debt financing, including a credit agreement with Citibank.

feed.title: Is It Worth Investing in Albemarle (ALB) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
feed.summary: Albemarle (ALB) currently has a "Buy"-equivalent average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.96, derived from 27 firms with 13 Strong Buy ratings. While analysts' recommendations can influence stock prices, their strong "Buy" bias due to vested interests may mislead investors. This article suggests using the ABR to validate the Zacks Rank, which is a more reliable predictor of stock price movement based on earnings estimate revisions, and notes that Albemarle currently holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: Insider Sell: Andrew Quigg Sells Shares of Progressive Corp (PGR)
feed.summary: Andrew Quigg, Chief Strategy Officer of Progressive Corp, sold 1,649 shares of the company on January 21, 2026. This transaction follows a pattern of 45 insider sells and no buys over the past year. Progressive Corp's stock is currently trading at $204.35, with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.79, suggesting it is modestly undervalued.

feed.title: Constellation Energy (CEG) stock slips as PJM price-cap talk collides with New York nuclear subsidy extension
feed.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) shares slipped due to conflicting news: New York extended nuclear subsidies until 2049, which benefits Constellation, but PJM officials proposed capping future auction prices and making data centers bear more grid expansion costs, which could negatively impact the company's profits. Analysts have also lowered fair value targets for CEG, and upcoming cold weather is expected to drive demand, creating further market volatility.

feed.title: Nomura’s Laser Digital Launches Tokenized Bitcoin Yield Fund for Accredited Investors
feed.summary: Nomura's digital asset arm, Laser Digital, has introduced the Bitcoin Diversified Yield Fund SP (BDYF), a tokenized Bitcoin yield fund aimed at accredited investors outside the US. The fund combines core Bitcoin holdings with market-neutral income strategies like arbitrage, lending, and options. It is structured as a natively tokenized Cayman fund, requiring a minimum subscription of $250,000, and is designed to meet institutional demand for regulated crypto yield products.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Stock Gained 1% Last Week After Q3 Sales Forecast Raised
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. (MCHP) stock rose by 6% early last week after the company raised its fiscal third-quarter net sales guidance to approximately $1.19 billion, exceeding its previous range. This uplift in outlook is attributed to improving bookings and backlog visibility, indicating a recalibration of short-term revenue expectations rather than a fundamental shift in long-term demand. A valuation model suggests a target price of $108.71, implying a potential 48.6% total return over 2.2 years, dependent on sustained recovery, margin normalization, and effective scaling of product lines.

feed.title: Enviri named a Global 100 Most Sustainable Corporation
feed.summary: Enviri, a Philadelphia-based environmental solutions provider, has been recognized for the first time on Corporate Knights' 2026 Global 100 Most Sustainable Corporations in the World list. The company ranked No. 55 overall and No. 2 in its industry category, with its CEO emphasizing their commitment to reducing waste and recovering materials. This recognition highlights Enviri's role in scaling sustainable solutions for environmental challenges globally.

feed.title: Thousands of Adirondack chairs sold at Lowe’s and Home Depot recalled
feed.summary: Approximately 6,100 Adirondack patio chairs sold at Lowe’s and Home Depot have been recalled due to injury and fall hazards. The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) stated that the chairs can crack and collapse, but no incidents or injuries have been reported so far. Consumers should stop using the chairs and return them to the stores for a full refund.

feed.title: Static interference begins the new year at Verizon Communications
feed.summary: Verizon Communications experienced a nationwide wireless network outage on January 14, affecting tens of thousands of users. In addition, the company settled a case for $7.7 million concerning environmental violations at hundreds of its cell towers in Southern California, with Ventura County agencies receiving over $1.14 million from the settlement. The violations involved hazardous materials and petroleum storage tanks, with authorities citing issues in reporting, training, and inspection access.

feed.title: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is inviting F5, Inc. (FFIV) shareholders who incurred losses between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, to join a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that F5 failed to disclose a significant security incident that put its clientele's security and the company's future prospects at risk, making their positive statements misleading. The lead plaintiff deadline for the lawsuit is February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial Stock Could Rally Back Near Record Peak
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial (SYF) stock, despite a recent dip in January 2026, is positioned for a potential rally back near its record peak. Technical analysis shows the stock has found support at the $75 level and is near a historically bullish trendline, with a 64% chance of a 5.5% gain in the next month based on past performance. Options traders are already bullish, and the stock's volatility is currently affordable.

feed.title: Derek Stevens Offers CAD Discounts to Attract Canadian Tourists Back to Las Vegas
feed.summary: Derek Stevens, owner of Golden Gate Hotel, The D Las Vegas, and Circa Resort & Casino, is offering Canadian tourists the ability to pay with Canadian dollars at par with U.S. dollars until August 31st. This initiative aims to revitalize Canadian tourism to Las Vegas, which saw a nearly 25% decline in 2024, by providing a significant discount on hotel rooms, bar purchases, and casino play. The promotion addresses a substantial drop in Canadian visitation and airline seat capacity, reflecting broader issues like rising travel costs and dissatisfaction with U.S. policies.

feed.title: How Exxon Mobil's Integrated Strengths Offset Crude Price Weakness
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's integrated business model and strong balance sheet allow it to navigate crude price volatility, even when prices are lower. The company leverages its refining operations to benefit from cheaper feedstock and is resilient to market fluctuations due to its low debt exposure. While its valuation is above the industry average, analysts have positively revised earnings estimates for 2025.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt (JBHT) Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 2.3%
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) has announced a 2.3% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.45 per share. While the company exhibits strong financial health indicators such as a high Z-Score, it faces challenges including slowing revenue growth, insider selling, and high valuation metrics. J.B. Hunt operates as a leading surface transportation provider in North America.

feed.title: IOBT IO Biotech, Inc. (NASDAQ) $0.47 on 22 Jan 2026: active intraday mover for traders
feed.summary: IO Biotech, Inc. (IOBT) experienced significant intraday trading volume on January 22, 2026, reaching $0.4663 per share, following the company's announcement to explore strategic alternatives. This news led to high volatility, with the stock closing down 9.56% intraday, and a substantial increase in trading volume. Meyka AI gives IOBT a "HOLD" rating with a projected one-year price of $0.5023, while highlighting the risks associated with the company's negative EPS and high price-to-book ratio.

feed.title: GM to move production of Buick SUV from China to US
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) announced it will move the production of its Buick Envision SUV from China to a U.S. assembly plant in the Kansas City area, starting in 2028. This decision aims to expand GM's domestic manufacturing footprint and support U.S. jobs, following years of 25% tariffs on the China-built Envision. The move is part of GM's broader strategy to increase U.S. factory work, which also includes shifting Chevrolet Equinox and Blazer production from Mexico to the U.S.

feed.title: Wilson Sonsini Advises Janux Therapeutics on Patent Matters Related to License Agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb
feed.summary: Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati advised Janux Therapeutics on patent matters for its collaboration and exclusive worldwide license agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb. This agreement involves the development of a tumor-activated therapeutic targeting a solid tumor antigen. Janux stands to receive up to $50 million in upfront and near-term milestone payments, potentially reaching approximately $800 million in total milestones, plus tiered royalties on global product sales.

feed.title: WEC Energy boosts payout 6.7%, dividend streak since 1942
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) has announced a 6.7% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it to $0.9525 per share, resulting in an annual dividend of $3.81 per share. This marks the 334th consecutive quarter of dividend payments since 1942 and the 23rd consecutive year of dividend increases. The company continues to target a dividend payout of 65-70% of earnings, demonstrating consistent returns for its stockholders.

feed.title: A Closer Look at Boston Scientific's Options Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Financial giants have shown significant interest in Boston Scientific (NYSE: BSX) options, with 21 unusual trades identified. The options activity suggests a predicted price range for BSX between $80.0 and $97.0 over the next three months. An analyst from Oppenheimer maintains an Outperform rating with a $100 price target, while the stock currently trades at $92.28.

feed.title: Amcor’s Slow-Burn Rebound: Is This Boring Packaging Stock Quietly Setting Up Its Next Move?
feed.summary: Amcor plc, a global packaging company, has been steadily trading in a tight range despite the broader market's focus on AI. The article suggests that while not a high-growth stock, Amcor presents an opportunity for patient, income-focused investors due to its strong cash flow, dividend yield, and strategic focus on sustainability and high-margin sectors. Analysts view Amcor as a defensive core holding with moderate upside potential, driven by operational excellence and a pivot towards eco-friendly packaging.

feed.title: General Motors to move Buick Envision SUV production from China to U.S.
feed.summary: General Motors announced plans to shift production of the Buick Envision SUV successor from China to its Fairfax Assembly plant in Kansas City, U.S., starting in 2028. This move aims to strengthen GM's domestic manufacturing footprint and support U.S. jobs, coming amidst stiff tariffs on imported vehicles and pressure for domestic investment. The company will continue to produce vehicles for international sale in China.

feed.title: Vestmark, T. Rowe Price team up for new RIA-focused model portfolios
feed.summary: Vestmark and T. Rowe Price have partnered to launch Custom Premier, a new model portfolio offering designed for RIAs. This collaboration aims to provide advisors with scaled personalization and tax management for clients, leveraging T. Rowe Price's investment expertise and Vestmark's tax-aware trading platform. The initiative addresses the growing demand from RIAs for customizable model portfolios that offer institutional oversight and risk management capabilities.

feed.title: OpenAI to help thousands of Leidos staff speed up federal work
feed.summary: Leidos and OpenAI have announced a partnership to integrate OpenAI's generative and agentic AI models into federal workflows across key sectors like national security, health services, and defense. This collaboration aims to enhance productivity and accelerate product development for government agencies, protecting customer data using secure configurations. Internally, Leidos will use ChatGPT and the OpenAI API to streamline tasks such as product design, automation, global threat assessments, supply chain monitoring, and deepfake detection, supporting its NorthStar 2030 strategy.

feed.title: Leidos, OpenAI deploying AI to transform federal operations
feed.summary: Leidos and OpenAI are partnering to integrate AI into federal operations, aiming to enhance efficiency and effectiveness within government agencies. This collaboration will deploy generative and agentic AI in strategic areas like digital modernization, health services, national security, and defense. Leidos employees will also leverage OpenAI's ChatGPT and API Platform to accelerate product development and improve internal automation.

feed.title: Public Service Enterprise cut at J.P. Morgan on New Jersey political uncertainty (PEG:NYSE)
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan downgraded Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) shares to Neutral from Overweight, reducing the price target to $85 from $88. The downgrade is primarily due to anticipated "persistent meaningful political and regulatory uncertainty" in New Jersey. This news saw PEG shares trade down by 0.5% on Thursday.

feed.title: Transocean (RIG) Hits Fresh High on Successful Drilling
feed.summary: Transocean (RIG) reached a new 52-week high after ConocoPhillips confirmed successful initial drilling in Australia's Otway Basin using Transocean's Equinox rig. The company also announced securing two new contracts worth $168 million for its Deepwater Mykonos and Transocean Enabler rigs, further boosting its backlog. Despite the positive news, the article concludes with a pitch for investing in AI stocks over RIG.

feed.title: Venezuela Opportunity Emerges as Oil Services Stocks Rally Through January
feed.summary: Oil services stocks, as represented by the VanEck Oil Services ETF (OIH), have rallied 18% year-to-date in January, driven by upcoming earnings reports from major holdings like Schlumberger and Baker Hughes. Despite a decline in WTI crude oil prices causing a 97% earnings drop for Halliburton in Q3 2025, the potential for a significant re-entry into Venezuela's oil fields—requiring an estimated $10 billion annually—presents a major opportunity for these service providers. Investors are closely watching earnings guidance and technological advancements, like Schlumberger's acquisition of RESMAN, to gauge the sector's future direction.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets (NASDAQ:CBOE) Reaches New 12-Month High - What's Next?
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets (NASDAQ:CBOE) recently hit a new 52-week high of $276.95, closing at $275.55 with significant trading volume. The company reported strong financial results, beating EPS estimates and achieving a 13.8% year-over-year revenue increase with a market capitalization of $28.99 billion. Analysts currently have an average "Hold" rating on CBOE, with a consensus price target of $268.36, and institutional investors have been actively trading its shares.

feed.title: Maxim Group Raises Nicolet Bankshares (NIC) Price Target to $173
feed.summary: Maxim Group has increased its price target for Nicolet Bankshares (NIC) to $173 from $161, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment reflects a 7.45% increase and indicates growing confidence in the stock's performance among analysts. Other firms like Piper Sandler and Keefe, Bruyette & Woods have also recently raised their price targets for NIC.

feed.title: P&G Stock Reaction Expected To Stay Muted After Mixed Q2
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) reported mixed Q2 results, with adjusted earnings beating estimates but revenue slightly missing. The company also lowered its fiscal 2026 GAAP earnings guidance, weighing on an otherwise solid quarter. Despite the trimmed outlook, Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating, expecting a muted market reaction due to margin strength and reiterated full-year organic sales and adjusted earnings outlook.

feed.title: Abu Dhabi merges CYVN's stake in Nio into new state-owned fund L'imad
feed.summary: Abu Dhabi has merged its holdings in Nio Inc and McLaren Automotive, previously under CYVN Holdings, into a new state-owned fund named L'imad Holding. This move centralizes investments and streamlines management, with a recent SEC filing showing L'imad holds a 17.9% stake in Nio. The fund is chaired by Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Progressive (NYSE:PGR) Insider Sells 1,649 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Andrew Quigg, an insider at Progressive (NYSE:PGR), sold 1,649 shares of the company's stock on January 21, 2026, for a total of $336,973.15, reducing his stake by 4%. The sale occurred at an average price of $204.35 per share. Progressive currently trades near $205.48, with a market cap of $120.49 billion and a P/E ratio of 11.27, while analysts hold a consensus "Hold" rating with a target price of $259.39.

feed.title: Blue Owl Capital Corporation upgraded to Baa2 by Moody’s
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded Blue Owl Capital Corporation's long-term issuer and senior unsecured ratings to Baa2 from Baa3, with a stable outlook. This upgrade reflects the company's strong management, robust underwriting and risk management capabilities, and expected reduction in leverage. Blue Owl Capital Corporation focuses on lending to upper middle market companies in less cyclical industries, maintaining substantial liquidity despite a modest increase in non-accrual investments.

feed.title: YUM Brands: Citigroup Maintains Neutral Rating, Raises Price Target
feed.summary: Citigroup has maintained a neutral rating on Yum Brands (YUM) while raising its price target from $164 to $170. This decision follows a series of diverse analyst actions, including upgrades and downgrades from other firms, highlighting varied perspectives on the company's market position. Yum Brands, the world's second-largest restaurant firm by dollar sales, operates largely through a franchised model and analysts project an average target price implying a 7.23% upside from its current price.

feed.title: LRCX: Citigroup Raises Price Target for Lam Research Amidst Maintained Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has significantly increased its price target for Lam Research (LRCX) to $265 from $190, while maintaining a Buy rating, reflecting a strong positive outlook. This follows similar upgrades from other firms like Deutsche Bank, Needham, UBS, RBC Capital, and Wells Fargo, underscoring robust market confidence in the semiconductor equipment manufacturer. Despite the analyst optimism, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 47.23% from the current price based on its GF Value fair value calculation.

feed.title: Trump Sues JPMorgan for Closing His Bank Accounts for Political Reasons
feed.summary: Donald Trump has filed a lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase and its CEO Jamie Dimon, seeking $5 billion in damages, alleging the bank closed his accounts for political reasons after the January 6th Capitol attack. JPMorgan stated its decision was not politically motivated and that the lawsuit has no merit, attributing account closures to legal or regulatory risk. The lawsuit claims Trump and his businesses were placed on a "blacklist" due to his conservative political views, echoing broader conservative concerns about "debanking."

feed.title: J. B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. Announces Increase to Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.45 per common share, representing a 2.3% increase from the previous dividend. This dividend is payable to stockholders of record on February 6, 2026, and will be paid on February 20, 2026. The company, known for its extensive transportation network in North America, aims to provide efficient solutions and enhance supply chain visibility for its customers.

feed.title: Billionaire Investor Doubles Down On AI, Boosts Stakes In Microsoft And Meta Despite 2025 Tech Volatility
feed.summary: Billionaire investor Dan Loeb's Third Point LLC significantly increased its stakes in Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, and Amazon in Q3 2025, betting heavily on artificial intelligence. Despite some underperformance in comparison to peers, these investments reflect Loeb's confidence in the long-term growth potential driven by AI advancements, cloud computing, and semiconductor demand. Microsoft is poised for a potential $5 trillion market cap, Meta is expanding AI and data centers, Nvidia's AI chip demand is skyrocketing, and AWS continues to be a strong performer for Amazon.

feed.title: GE Aerospace outlook beats estimates
feed.summary: GE Aerospace has forecasted full-year profit that exceeds Wall Street expectations, leading to a 1.4% rise in its shares in premarket trading. The company anticipates that strong demand for air travel will help mitigate broader economic concerns. Adjusted earnings are projected to be between $7.10 and $7.40 per share this year, surpassing the average analyst estimate of $7.10.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt raises quarterly dividend by 2.3% to $0.45 per share
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. announced a 2.3% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.45 per common share, marking the 12th consecutive year of dividend increases for the company. The dividend is payable on February 20, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 6, 2026. This move highlights J.B. Hunt's consistent financial performance and commitment to shareholder returns.
